children.heading: Youths, students, and persons with disabilities
children.label: A Youths, students, and persons with disabilities
i
children.text: who is under 18 years of age; or

children.label: I
children.text: 18 years of age or older; and

children.label: II
children.text: a person with disabilities or a full-time student.

children.heading: Elderly and disabled families
children.label: B Elderly and disabled families
children.text: $400 for an elderly or disabled family.

children.label: i
children.text: medical expenses, in the case of an elderly or disabled family; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reasonable attendant care and auxiliary apparatus expenses for each family member who is a person with disabilities, to the extent necessary to enable any member of the family (including a member who is a person with disabilities) to be employed.

children.heading: Child care expenses
children.label: D Child care expenses
children.text: Child care expenses, to the extent necessary to enable another member of the family to be employed or to further his or her education.

children.heading: Earned income of minors
children.label: E Earned income of minors
children.text: The amount of any earned income of any member of the family who is less than 18 years of age.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: F Travel expenses
children.text: Excessive travel expenses, not to exceed $25 per family per week, for employment- or education-related travel.

children.heading: Other amounts
children.label: G Other amounts
children.text: Such other amounts as may be provided in the Indian housing plan for an Indian tribe.

children.heading: Affordable housing
children.label: 2 Affordable housing
children.text: The term “affordable housing” means housing that complies with the requirements for affordable housing under subchapter II. The term includes permanent housing for homeless persons who are persons with disabilities, transitional housing, and single room occupancy housing.

children.heading: Drug-related criminal activity
children.label: 3 Drug-related criminal activity
children.text: The term “drug-related criminal activity” means the illegal manufacture, sale, distribution, use, or possession with intent to manufacture, sell, distribute, or use, of a controlled substance (as such term is defined in ).section 802 of title 21

children.heading: Elderly families and near-elderly families
children.label: 4 Elderly families and near-elderly families
children.text: The terms “elderly family” and “near-elderly family” mean a family whose head (or his or her spouse), or whose sole member, is an elderly person or a near-elderly person, respectively. Such terms include 2 or more elderly persons or near-elderly persons living together, and 1 or more such persons living with 1 or more persons determined under the Indian housing plan for the agency to be essential to their care or well-being.

children.heading: Elderly person
children.label: 5 Elderly person
children.text: The term “elderly person” means a person who is at least 62 years of age.

children.heading: Family
children.label: 6 Family
children.text: The term “family” includes a family with or without children, an elderly family, a near-­elderly family, a disabled family, and a single person.

children.heading: Grant beneficiary
children.label: 7 Grant beneficiary
children.text: The term “grant beneficiary” means the Indian tribe or tribes on behalf of which a grant is made under this chapter to a recipient.

children.label: i
children.text: is owned by an Indian tribe or a tribally designated housing entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: is necessary to the provision of housing in an Indian area; and

children.label: I
children.text: would help an Indian tribe or tribally designated housing entity to reduce the cost of construction of Indian housing;

children.label: II
children.text: would make housing more affordable, accessible, or practicable in an Indian area; or

children.label: III
children.text: would otherwise advance the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: The term “housing and community development” does not include any activity conducted by any Indian tribe under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 2701

children.label: A
children.text: Any amounts not actually received by the family.

children.label: B
children.text: Any amounts that would be eligible for exclusion under .section 1382b(a)(7) of title 42

children.label: C
children.text: Any amounts received by any member of the family as disability compensation under chapter 11 of title 38 or dependency and indemnity compensation under chapter 13 of such title.

children.heading: Indian
children.label: 10 Indian
children.text: The term “Indian” means any person who is a member of an Indian tribe.

children.heading: Indian area
children.label: 11 Indian area
children.text: The term “Indian area” means the area within which an Indian tribe or a tribally designated housing entity, as authorized by 1 or more Indian tribes, provides assistance under this chapter for affordable housing.

children.heading: Indian housing plan
children.label: 12 Indian housing plan
children.text: The term “Indian housing plan” means a plan under .section 4112 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means a tribe that is a federally recognized tribe or a State recognized tribe.

children.heading: Federally recognized tribe
children.label: B Federally recognized tribe
children.text: The term “federally recognized tribe” means any Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community of Indians, including any Alaska Native village or regional or village corporation as defined in or established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.], that is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians pursuant to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.).43 U.S.C. 160125 U.S.C. 4501See References in Text note below.1

children.label: I
children.text: that has been recognized as an Indian tribe by any State; and

children.label: II
children.text: for which an Indian Housing Authority has, before the effective date under section 705, entered into a contract with the Secretary pursuant to the United States Housing Act of 1937 [ et seq.] for housing for Indian families and has received funding pursuant to such contract within the 5-year period ending upon such effective date.42 U.S.C. 1437

children.label: I
children.text: the allocation formula under shall be determined for a State recognized tribe under tribal membership eligibility criteria in existence on ; andsection 4152 of this titleOctober 26, 1996

children.label: II
children.text: nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to confer upon a State recognized tribe any rights, privileges, responsibilities, or obligations otherwise accorded groups recognized as Indian tribes by the United States for other purposes.

children.heading: Low-income family
children.label: 14 Low-income family
children.text: The term “low-income family” means a family whose income does not exceed 80 percent of the median income for the area, as determined by the Secretary with adjustments for smaller and larger families, except that the Secretary may, for purposes of this paragraph, establish income ceilings higher or lower than 80 percent of the median for the area on the basis of the findings of the Secretary or the agency that such variations are necessary because of prevailing levels of construction costs or unusually high or low family incomes.

children.label: A
children.text: the median income for the Indian area, which the Secretary shall determine; or

children.label: B
children.text: the median income for the United States.

children.heading: Near-elderly person
children.label: 16 Near-elderly person
children.text: The term “near-elderly person” means a person who is at least 55 years of age and less than 62 years of age.

children.heading: Nonprofit
children.label: 17 Nonprofit
children.text: The term “nonprofit” means, with respect to an organization, association, corporation, or other entity, that no part of the net earnings of the entity inures to the benefit of any member, founder, contributor, or individual.

children.label: A
children.text: has a disability as defined in ;section 423 of title 42

children.label: i
children.text: is expected to be of long-continued and indefinite duration;

children.label: ii
children.text: substantially impedes his or her ability to live independently; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is of such a nature that such ability could be improved by more suitable housing conditions; or

children.label: C
children.text: has a developmental disability as defined in .section 15002 of title 42

children.heading: Recipient
children.label: 19 Recipient
children.text: The term “recipient” means an Indian tribe or the entity for one or more Indian tribes that is authorized to receive grant amounts under this chapter on behalf of the tribe or tribes.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 20 Secretary
children.text: Except as otherwise specifically provided in this chapter, the term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.heading: State
children.label: 21 State
children.text: The term “State” means the States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, the Virgin Islands, American Samoa, and any other territory or possession of the United States and Indian tribes.

children.label: i
children.text: was established for purposes of the United States Housing Act of 1937 [ et seq.] before , that meets the requirements under the United States Housing Act of 1937,42 U.S.C. 1437October 26, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: is acting on , as the Indian housing authority for the tribe, andOctober 26, 1996

children.label: iii
children.text: is not an Indian tribe for purposes of this chapter,

children.label: i
children.text: by exercise of the power of self-government of one or more Indian tribes independent of State law, or

children.label: ii
children.text: by operation of State law providing specifically for housing authorities or housing entities for Indians, including regional housing authorities in the State of Alaska,

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: C Establishment
children.text: A tribally designated housing entity may be authorized or established by one or more Indian tribes to act on behalf of each such tribe authorizing or establishing the housing entity.
